mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of months with PAYEMS growth > 50k.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates a tightening cycle that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals strong supply-side momentum that supports corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative when both INDPRO and GDPC1 show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: Residential sales (RSAFS) above 350k and housing starts (HOUST) above 2000k indicate strong demand for housing, boosting construction sector performance and generating positive S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when both RSAFS > 350k and HOUST > 2000k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity that increases corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative despite INDPRO growth > 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A CPI inflation rate below 2.0% suggests accommodative monetary policy, reducing real interest rates and boosting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when CPIAUCSL < 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE) and increases in housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong labor market and housing demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when UNRATE declines and HOUST rises.

mechanism_narrative: A recessionary regime characterized by rising unemployment (UNRATE) and declining residential sales (RSAFS) reduces consumer confidence and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive during periods of rising UNRATE and declining RSAFS.
